sourceMovie.title: Mary Poppins
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Mary Poppins works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Mary Poppins Returns
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Mary Poppins Returns (2018) is the natural next step after Mary Poppins: same comedy DNA, different perspective. The funny streak and family elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Addams Family Values
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Mary Poppins who respond to its comedy will find Addams Family Values (1993) immediately familiar. Both films share a family sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Nanny McPhee
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Mary Poppins fans keep landing on Nanny McPhee (2005) — the comedy is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Roald Dahl's Matilda the Musical
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Roald Dahl's Matilda the Musical draws from the same comedy well as Mary Poppins, channeling family in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2022 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Spellbound
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Spellbound (2024) captures the comedy appeal of Mary Poppins without simply imitating it. The family streak running through both films is what puts Spellbound at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Spirited Away
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The family appeal of Mary Poppins — that uplifting edge, the fantasy — translates cleanly into Spirited Away (2001). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Groundhog Day
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Groundhog Day (1993) clearly understood what makes comedy work — the same quality that keeps Mary Poppins rewatchable. Expect the same funny rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: The Simpsons Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Simpsons Movie (2007) is the kind of film Mary Poppins fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The comedy overlap is real, but it's the funny emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: The Little Mermaid
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Little Mermaid shares Mary Poppins's gift for family — the 1989 film delivers fantasy without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Cinderella
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Mary Poppins compelling — the family, the fantasy — shows up in Cinderella (1950) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that uplifting storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

faq.question: What should I watch after Mary Poppins?
faq.answer: Try Mary Poppins Returns, Addams Family Values, Nanny McPhee—each shares DNA with Mary Poppins while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Mary Poppins?
faq.answer: Mary Poppins (1964) is best described as Comedy, Family, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Mary Poppins part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Mary Poppins Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
